papers.title: The Tool-Overuse Illusion: Why Does LLM Prefer External Tools over Internal Knowledge?
papers.innovation: Reveals and characterizes the pervasive problem of 'tool overuse' in LLMs, where models unnecessarily opt for external tools even when internal knowledge suffices. This understanding is crucial for designing more efficient and reliable LLM agents.
papers.benchmarks: Implies evaluation across diverse LLMs to demonstrate the pervasiveness of the phenomenon, likely involving agent-based tasks where tool calls are a metric.
papers.use_case: Designing more efficient and robust LLM agents, optimizing resource allocation by preventing unnecessary tool API calls, and improving the overall decision-making quality of AI systems that integrate external tools.

papers.title: Explainable AML Triage with LLMs: Evidence Retrieval and Counterfactual Checks
papers.innovation: Proposes a method for explainable AML triage using LLMs that addresses auditability and governance constraints. Key innovations include structured output, evidence retrieval, and counterfactual checks to mitigate risks of unconstrained generation.
papers.benchmarks: Evaluated on real-world AML alert triage datasets, focusing on metrics related to explanation quality, auditability, and decision accuracy within a regulated environment.
papers.use_case: Deploying LLMs in high-stakes, regulated industries (e.g., finance, healthcare) where interpretability, auditability, and control over model generation are paramount for compliance and trust. Specifically, for building explainable AI systems for fraud detection and risk assessment.

papers.title: Inference Headroom Ratio: A Diagnostic and Control Framework for Inference Stability Under Constraint
papers.innovation: Introduces the 'Inference Headroom Ratio' (IHR), a dimensionless diagnostic quantity, and a control framework for characterizing and managing inference stability in constrained decision systems. This formalizes the relationship between inferential capacity and uncertainty.
papers.benchmarks: Simulation-based evaluation is used to demonstrate the effectiveness of IHR in diagnosing and controlling inference stability under various system constraints and uncertainties.
papers.use_case: Designing, monitoring, and optimizing robust and reliable AI inference systems in production environments. Crucial for MLOps engineers to ensure systems maintain stability and performance under varying operational conditions and resource limitations.
